codebase.readTools: `Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` ŌĆö read files, search for patterns, follow imports
codebase.readInstruction: Pick an area ŌĆö a directory, a cluster of related files, whatever looks important or uncovered. Read 3-8 files together. Follow imports and callers. Understand the area, don't just skim.
codebase.deepReadInstruction: Open a file, read it line by line, compare it to the destination artifact that claims to cover it. You will find missing details, wrong claims, or oversimplified descriptions. Spot-checking file names against artifact titles is not reading.
codebase.tip: Read related files together ŌĆö a module without its imports is half the picture.

filesystem.readTools: `Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` ŌĆö read files, search for patterns
filesystem.readInstruction: Pick a cluster of related files. Read them together for context.
filesystem.deepReadInstruction: Open a file, read it fully, compare it to the destination artifact that claims to cover it.
filesystem.tip: Read related files together for context.

graph.readTools: `memstead_entity` (pass `include_relations:true` to follow typed edges, `include_context:true` for cluster context), `memstead_search` (omit `query` for pure structural/metadata filtering) ŌĆö read entity content, follow relationships
graph.readInstruction: Pick a cluster of related entities. Read their content and relationships. Understand what each entity actually says, not just its title.
graph.deepReadInstruction: Read an entity's full content section by section. Compare it to the destination artifact that claims to represent it. Check every claim.
graph.tip: Read entity content, not just titles ŌĆö understand what each entity actually says.

git.readInstruction: Read commit messages and diffs together. Group related commits by topic ŌĆö a feature or fix often spans multiple commits. Follow the reasoning trail: why was this change made, what was the alternative?
git.deepReadInstruction: Run `git show` on the commit. Read the full diff. Compare it to the destination artifact that claims to capture this decision. Check whether the reasoning and consequences are accurately described.
git.tip: Commit messages capture intent. Diffs capture what actually changed. Read both ŌĆö the message alone is often incomplete.

web.readTools: `WebSearch`, `WebFetch`, `Read` ŌĆö search the web, fetch pages, read content
web.readInstruction: Research the topic from multiple angles. Triangulate across sources ŌĆö no single page tells the full story.
web.deepReadInstruction: Re-read the source page carefully. Compare it to the destination artifact that claims to cover it.
web.tip: Triangulate across multiple sources ŌĆö no single page tells the full story.

graph.writeTools: `memstead_create`, `memstead_update`, `memstead_delete`, `memstead_relate` ŌĆö modify the graph (loop `memstead_update` for multi-entity edits)
graph.exploreTools: `memstead_search` (omit `query` for pure structural/metadata filtering), `memstead_entity` (with `include_relations:true` / `include_context:true` for edges and cluster context), `memstead_overview` (token-budget-driven cold-start ŌĆö schema catalogue lists `{ref, description}` only), `memstead_schema(name=<ref>)` (full per-type bodies ŌĆö sections, fields, write_rules, relationship vocabulary; cache per session) ŌĆö explore the graph
graph.writeInstruction: One entity per concept ŌĆö not per source artifact, not per code layer. Search the graph before creating ŌĆö update existing entities, don't duplicate. Wire relationships as you go.
graph.tip: The graph is your memory from previous runs. Understand it before changing it.

codebase.writeInstruction: Read existing files before overwriting ŌĆö preserve what still applies.
codebase.tip: Read existing files before overwriting ŌĆö preserve what still applies.

filesystem.writeInstruction: Read existing files before overwriting ŌĆö preserve what still applies.
filesystem.tip: Read existing files before overwriting ŌĆö preserve what still applies.
